title: Wear detection apparatus for a comminution device
abstract: A comminution apparatus can include a wear component. The wear component can be coupled to a wear detector so that measurements of the liner can be taken to detect a current state of wear of the liner. The detection of the worn state may occur at discrete time intervals (e.g. every day, every 30 mins, etc.). In some embodiments, the wear detector can be configured so that it is powered wirelessly via a wireless transmission of an electromagnetic field or a magnetic field that is generated by a wireless power source mechanism positioned away from a crushing body for wireless transmission of power to the wear detector. The wear detector can also be configured to wireless communicate data relating to the wear measurements to at least one remote computer device via a wireless local area network connection or other type of wireless communication connection.
claims: Claims Claims 1 . A comminution apparatus comprising: an energy transfer unit configured to generate a magnetic field; a moveable body attached to a wearable surface such that the moveable body and the wearable surface or moveable within the magnetic field; at least one sensor within the wearable surface configured generate data that is utilizable to measure at least one property of the wearable surface relating to an extent to which the wearable surface has experienced wear, the at least one sensor configured such that the magnetic field wirelessly induces a voltage that powers the at least one sensor. 2. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the moveable body and the wearable surface rotate in the magnetic field. 3. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the wearable surface is a liner and the moveable body is a shell and the comminution apparatus is configured as a Semi- Autogenous Grinding (SAG) mill. 4. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 , comprising: at least one access point connected to the wearable surface and the at least one sensor; the at least one access point having a coil of wire that generates a voltage to power the access point when the wearable surface is moved within the magnetic field. 5. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the at least one sensor is electrically connected to a coil of wire that generates a voltage to generate the data. 6. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 , comprising: at least one transceiver unit configured to wirelessly communicate with the at least one sensor to wirelessly transmit wear data of the at least one sensor to a remote computer device 7. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the at least one sensor is comprised of a sensor that have a plurality of pads and generate data by detecting an amount of resistance or capacitance to electrical current that occurs form a current being passed through the pads. 8. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the at least one sensor is comprised of a first sensor that has a light emitting device. 9. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 , wherein said wearable surface is a liner, said liner comprising a sensor to detect wear, said sensor configured to detect a change in thickness and/or angular position and/or strain and/or mass and/or time and/or a battery of Hall- or magnetic resistance sensor on or in said wearable surface. 1 0. The comminution apparatus of claim 9, wherein said sensor is configured to detect wear in at least 4, such as 8 such as 16 discrete steps, with or without use of a signal amplification and conditioning. 1 1 . The comminution apparatus of claim 9 or 10, further comprising communication means to communicate from said sensor to outside of said comminution apparatus, said communication is performed by a wire, or by a two-step wireless communication mean or a combination of wired and wireless communication mean. 1 2. The comminution apparatus of claims 9-1 1 , wherein said sensor is powered by a battery. 1 3. The comminution apparatus of claims 1 1 -1 2, wherein said communication means is powered by a battery. 14. The comminution apparatus of claims 1 1 -1 3, wherein said two-step wireless communication mean is powered by a battery. 1 5. The comminution apparatus of claims 1 1 -14, wherein said two step wireless communication mean is an antenna. 1 6. The comminution apparatus of claims 15, wherein said antenna is an integrated antenna in said liner. 1 7. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 5 or 16, wherein said antenna is a sacrificial antenna. 1 8. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 7, wherein said sacrificial antenna is placed in a waveguide. 1 9. The comminution apparatus of claim 1 7 or 1 8, wherein said sacrificial antenna functions as a wear sensor. 20. The comminution apparatus of claims 1 1 -19, wherein said sensor is arranged on a side of said liner, said sensor being arranged in a swallow tail groove and fastened by means of a covering metal part and fixtures. 21 . The comminution apparatus of claims 1 1 -20, wherein said two step wireless communication mean is a plurality of antennas. 22. A method of automatically monitoring wear of a wearable surface of a comminution apparatus comprising: attaching at least one sensor within or on a wearable surface, each sensor configured to generate data that measures at least one property of the wearable surface relating to an extent to which the wearable surface has experienced wear; attaching the wearable surface to a moveable body; attaching at least one transceiver unit to the moveable body, the at least one transceiver unit configured to wirelessly communicate with the at least one sensor to wirelessly transmit wear data of the at least one sensor to a remote computer device; and rotating the moveable body and the wearable surface within a magnetic field to wirelessly power the at least one sensor and the at least one transceiver unit to power the at least one sensor to generate the data and to power the at least one transceiver unit to facilitate wireless transmission of the generated data to the remote computer device. 23. The method of claim 22, comprising: attaching at least one access point to the wearable surface such that the at least one access point is communicatively connectable to the at least one sensor and the at least one transceiver unit; wherein the rotating the moveable body and the wearable surface within the magnetic field also powers the at least one access point to facilitate transmission of the generated data from the at least one sensor to the at least one transceiver unit.
